title: Childcare costs not entered — 2 children in household
explanation: Théo (age 5) and Lucie (age 2) are listed in the household but monthly childcare cost is $0. DC daycare averages $1,800–$2,200/month per child. Even partial childcare expenses are a dollar-for-dollar SNAP deduction that directly increases the monthly benefit.
suggestedAction: Ask Marie-Claire or Nadège: Do they pay for any daycare, after-school program, or babysitter so they can work? Document receipts or provider name.

title: Medical deduction may be understated — diabetic household member
explanation: Notes indicate Marie-Claire (age 68) has Type 2 diabetes, but only $180/month in medical expenses is recorded. DC Medicaid copays, insulin supplies, transportation to medical appointments, and diabetic supplies all qualify. The medical deduction applies to all out-of-pocket expenses over $35/month for elderly or disabled members.
suggestedAction: Request pharmacy receipts and medical appointment records. Any expense above $35/month increases SNAP eligibility dollar-for-dollar.
